channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: YouTube is cracking down on gambling content. Here’s what’s changing
item.description: YouTube is taking steps to crack down on gambling content. On Tuesday, the platform announced a new policy that bans creators from directing viewers to “unapproved” gambling websites through links, images, text, logos, or verbal mentions.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Microsoft reportedly struggling to build its own reasoning models to rival OpenAI
item.description: Microsoft may be “all in” with AI, but its efforts to produce homegrown products to feed its ambitions have so far been lagging and its reliance on OpenAI prevails. However, recent events may augur change.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: NordVPN reacts to results from its latest security audit
item.description: The independent security check conucted by Cure53 comes only a few weeks after a successful no-log audit. Here's all you need to know.
item.dc:creator: chiara.castro@futurenet.com (Chiara Castro), TechRadar

item.title: AMD's new driver adds AFMF 2.1 support for improved frame generation - and it could be a game-changer for handheld gaming PCs
item.description: FSR 4 might be incredible for RDNA 4 GPU users, but AMD's new AFMF 2.1 could be just as exciting for handheld gamers.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Android 16 beta users are reporting major battery drain issues – but I’m not too worried about it
item.description: The latest Android 16 beta seems to be putting increased stress on phones’ battery life, with some users reporting the need to charge up twice a day – but with a stable release still a ways off, there’s time for Google to fix it.
item.dc:creator: jamie.richards@futurenet.com (Jamie Richards), TechRadar

item.title: United Airlines successfully installed Starlink on its first aircraft, and the promised speeds are up to 50 times faster
item.description: United Airlines has successfully installed Starlink on its first regional aircraft and is promising Wi-Fi speeds up to 250MBps, which is 50 times faster than what you currently get.
item.dc:creator: jacob.krol@futurenet.com (Jacob Krol), TechRadar

item.title: The Google TV Streamer is now finally worth buying in the UK
item.description: Google's little streamer finally fixes its biggest problem by adding iPlayer and BBC streaming support.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Stop carrying your physical driver's license and add it to your Apple Wallet
item.description: A digital ID may be even more secure than a physical ID. Storing your driver's license in your Apple Wallet is an ideal solution for commuters.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 7 Slack apps for file sharing and collaboration
item.description: Slack is a powerful tool for collaborative chatting among co-workers, as well as brainstorming, task management, and more.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump signs executive order to establish government bitcoin reserve
item.description: President Donald Trump has signed an executive order establishing a government reserve of bitcoin, a key marker in the cryptocurrency’s journey towards possible mainstream acceptance.
item.dc:creator: Alan Suderman, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: HPE cuts 2,500 jobs, expects Juniper buy to close year-end ’25, faces tariff issues
item.description: Despite a mostly positive revenue report for the first quarter of its fiscal 2025, HPE executives detailed a number of challenges the company will face in the coming months, including layoffs, a court case over its proposed buy of Juniper Networks, and the U.S. government’s tariff plan.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Siemens announces $285 million investment in US manufacturing
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Siemens plans to invest $285 million in manufacturing in the United States, the tech company said Thursday, including two new facilities in California and Texas. The U.S. is the largest market for the company. The recent investments in the Siemens' U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation
item.description: All components of a zero trust architecture must generate data that is observable, available and able to be correlated within a unified data plane. The post Achieving unified visibility for effective zero trust implementation first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Chrome Enterprise moves to better differentiate work, personal browsing
item.description: Google on Thursday unveiled new security controls for Chrome Enterprise, allowing IT departments to help employees better distinguish between work and personal browsing profiles.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: House Judiciary panel subpoenas Google, YouTube parent company
item.description: House Judiciary Committee Chair Jim Jordan (R-Ohio) on Thursday subpoenaed Alphabet, the parent company of Google and YouTube, demanding the tech giant turn over its communications with the executive branch.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Senate Democrat slams Trump's 'grift' meme coins
item.description: Sen. Chris Murphy (D-Conn.) slammed President Trump for his launch of a meme coin in January, suggesting it might be the "biggest White House corruption in a century.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The Trump administration just cut Defense Department grants that research terrorism and drug trafficking
item.description: Researchers in a highly regarded Department of Defense program called the Minerva Research Initiative recently received word that grants already awarded by the Defense Department are being terminated, potentially putting their work—and in some cases even paychecks for their students and lab staff—in
item.dc:creator: Steven Melendez, Fast Company

item.title: Top network and data center events 2025
item.description: Ready to travel to gain hands-on experience with new networking and infrastructure tools? Tech conferences – in person and virtual – give attendees a chance to access product demos, network with peers, earn continuing education credits, and catch a celebrity keynote or live entertainment.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Without being there, Apple hits Mobile World Congress
item.description: If you overlook the introduction of the iPhone 16e with its own Apple-designed 5G modem along with the release of new iPads, Macs and processors, you might argue that Apple didn’t make an impression at this year’s Mobile World Congress.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Tax scam alert: How to protect yourself and your tax refund
item.description: A cybersecurity firm has shed some light on just how common tax scams have become. Kurt the CyberGuy offers his advice for how to protect your refund.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Albania shuts down TikTok for a year amid concerns over violence among children
item.description: The Albanian Cabinet has decided to shut down TikTok for 12 months, blaming the video-sharing platform for inciting violence and bullying, especially among children.
item.dc:creator: By Llazar Semini, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Apple appeals UK encryption backdoor demand
item.description: Apple has publicly been pushing back against a UK order that demands it create encryption backdoors, and it has now formally appealed that order to the UK Investigatory Powers Tribunal.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: C++ founder champions profiles for memory safety
item.description: C++ memory safety issues have come under heat recently, with rising interest in projects like TrapC and Safe C++ that attempt to tackle the issue. But C++ designer Bjarne Stroustrup remains steadfast in promoting profiles as essential for safety in C++.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Microsoft launches genAI sales agents, also focuses on finance and supply chain
item.description: Microsoft is planning a string of new AI agents to automate mundane tasks and improve worker productivity. The company on Wednesday announced two AI-based sales agents that can autonomously follow up on sales leads.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Trump Slams CHIPS Act, Proposes Tariffs Over Funding - CyberGuy
item.description: President Donald J. Trump called the CHIPS Act 'horrible' during his address to Congress, proposing instead to use leftover funds to reduce the national debt.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Judge denies Elon Musk's request to block OpenAI for-profit conversion but welcomes trial
item.description: OAKLAND, Calif. (AP) — A federal judge has denied Elon Musk's request for a court order blocking OpenAI from converting itself to a for-profit company but said she could expedite a trial to consider Musk's claims against the ChatGPT maker and its CEO. U.S.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and BARBARA ORTUTAY, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: UK watchdog drops competition review of Microsoft's OpenAI deal
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Britain's competition watchdog said Wednesday it has dropped its review of Microsoft's partnership with OpenAI, saying it's satisfied that the deal doesn't need a closer investigation under the country's merger rules.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Salesforce’s Agentforce 2dx update aims to simplify AI agent development, deployment
item.description: Salesforce on Wednesday released the third version of its agentic AI offering — Agentforce 2dx — to simplify the development, testing, and deployment of AI agents that can automate business processes across departments, such as sales, service, marketing, finance, HR, and operations.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: AI pioneers who channeled 'hedonistic' machines win computer science's top prize
item.description: Teaching machines in the way that animal trainers mold the behavior of dogs or horses has been an important method for developing artificial intelligence and one that was recognized Wednesday with the top computer science award.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Why you should use dependency injection
item.description: A few weeks ago, I wrote about how a good software team will defer decisions as long as possible. Smart teams will design and build systems that don’t lock them into any particular implementation until it is utterly necessary.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Top data storage certifications to sharpen your skills
item.description: Enterprise data storage skills are in demand, and that means storage certifications can be more valuable to organizations looking for people with those qualifications.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian joins bid to acquire TikTok
item.description: Reddit co-founder Alexis Ohanian has joined billionaire Frank McCourt’s bid to acquire TikTok as a strategic adviser.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: USDA’s automations taking some of the burden out of HR work
item.description: Brian Mohr, USDA’s the enterprise automation program manager, said over the past year, the agency developed 15 new automations mostly for HR processes. The post USDA’s automations taking some of the burden out of HR work first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Minnesota considers blocking 'nudify' apps that use AI to make explicit images without consent
item.description: ST. PAUL, Minn. (AP) — Molly Kelley was stunned to discover in June that someone she knew had used widely available “nudification” technology to create highly realistic and sexually explicit videos and images of her, using family photos that were posted on social media.
item.dc:creator: STEVE KARNOWSKI, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Employee screening data breach exposes 3.3 million records
item.description: Major employee screening company data breach affects 3.3 million people. Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson explains what happened and gives five ways to protect your data.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Improving intelligent document processing with generative AI
item.description: One of the earliest digital transformation drivers was digitalization, in which organizations converted paper-based processes to digitized workflows. In ideal circumstances, paper documents became web or mobile forms, and workflows replaced handoffs.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office
item.description: Thomas Shedd, the commissioner of Federal Acquisition Service, said the decision is part of the President’s Workforce Optimization Initiative executive order. The post After rocky history, GSA shuts down 18F office first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network
